body:
## Summary

This PR addresses issue #1 by implementing comprehensive improvements to the VEIL VPN GUI clients:

### Changes Made

1. **Cryptographic Settings Section in GUI**
   - Added UI for selecting `client.key` (pre-shared key file) path
   - Added UI for selecting `obfuscation.seed` file path  
   - Both fields include file browser buttons and validation
   - Settings are now persisted using QSettings for cross-session storage

2. **TUN Interface Configuration**
   - Device name configuration (default: veil0)
   - IP address with validation (default: 10.8.0.2)
   - Netmask with validation (default: 255.255.255.0)
   - MTU spinner with range 576-65535 (default: 1400)
   - Matches CLI client configuration options

3. **System Tray Integration**
   - System tray icon shows current connection state
   - Tray menu with quick connect/disconnect actions
   - Status display in tooltip and menu
   - Minimize to tray on window close
   - Click tray icon to toggle window visibility

4. **Keyboard Shortcuts**
   - `Ctrl+1`: Connection view
   - `Ctrl+2`: Settings view
   - `Ctrl+3`: Diagnostics view
   - `Ctrl+M`: Minimize to tray
   - `Ctrl+Enter`: Quick connect
   - `Ctrl+D`: Quick disconnect
   - `F1`: About dialog

5. **Application Icons** *(Enhanced)*
   - Created polished SVG icons for all connection states with gradients and glow effects:
     - `icon_disconnected.svg` - Gray shield outline with subtle depth
     - `icon_connecting.svg` - Blue/cyan shield with glow and loading indicators
     - `icon_connected.svg` - Green filled shield with checkmark badge
     - `icon_error.svg` - Red shield with error indicator badge
   - Icons follow the VEIL design language with shield motif and "V" letter

6. **Smooth Page Transition Animations**
   - Custom `AnimatedStackedWidget` class for fade transitions between views
   - 250ms smooth fade in/out animations using Qt property animations
   - Proper cleanup of graphics effects after transitions

7. **Auto-Update Checker** *(NEW)*
   - Checks GitHub Releases API for new versions on startup
   - Manual check via Help → "Check for Updates" menu
   - Shows dialog with version info and download link when update available
   - Semver comparison supporting prerelease suffixes

8. **Installer Upgrade Support (NSIS)** *(NEW)*
   - Detects existing installations and offers upgrade or fresh install
   - Version comparison prevents accidental downgrades
   - Option to backup settings before upgrade
   - Stops running instances before upgrade
   - Preserves QSettings during upgrade process
   - Version metadata embedded in installer executable
   - Russian language support added

9. **Comprehensive Release Workflow** *(NEW)*
   - New `release.yml` that builds Windows and Linux artifacts
   - Creates Windows installer with NSIS
   - Creates Linux DEB packages and tarballs
   - Generates changelog from git history
   - Creates draft GitHub releases with all assets

10. **Linux Build Workflow**
    - Full CI/CD for Ubuntu 22.04 (Focal) and Ubuntu 24.04 (Noble)
    - Builds client, server, and GUI applications
    - Runs unit tests in CI
    - Generates DEB packages for releases
    - Creates portable tarball archives

### Technical Details

- Settings widget uses `QSettings` for persistent configuration storage
- File path validation checks that files exist and are readable before connecting
- CMakeLists.txt updated to include Qt resource compilation
- Optional Qt6::Svg module linked when available
- System tray uses QSystemTrayIcon for cross-platform support
- Keyboard shortcuts use QShortcut and QKeySequence
- Page transitions use QGraphicsOpacityEffect with parallel animations
- New `src/common/version.h` for centralized version constants
- New `UpdateChecker` class in GUI for automatic update detection

### Server-Client Pipeline Evaluation

The current pipeline architecture for the VEIL VPN application follows a solid design:
- **Client-Server Communication**: The CLI client connects to the server using UDP with encrypted tunneling
- **TUN Interface**: Both client and server create virtual network interfaces for traffic routing
- **Signal Handling**: Proper signal handlers for graceful shutdown (SIGINT, SIGTERM)
- **Route Management**: Automatic route table modifications with cleanup on disconnect
- **Configuration**: Flexible configuration via command-line args, config files, and QSettings

The GUI communicates with an IPC daemon (ipc_client_manager) which handles the connection state and metrics. This separation allows for:
- Non-blocking UI updates
- Proper privilege separation (GUI doesn't need root/admin)
- Clean state management between components

### Test Plan

- [x] Build passes on Linux with Clang
- [x] GUI launches and displays the connection widget
- [x] Settings page shows the Cryptographic Settings section
- [x] Settings page shows the TUN Interface section
- [x] File browser dialogs open correctly for key and seed files
- [x] Settings persist across application restarts
- [x] Window displays the VEIL shield icon
- [x] System tray icon appears and updates with connection state
- [x] Keyboard shortcuts navigate between views
- [x] Page transitions animate smoothly
- [x] Auto-update checker compiles and integrates with mainwindow
- [x] NSIS installer script syntax is valid
- [ ] CI workflow runs successfully on pull request (requires maintainer approval)
- [ ] Windows build with installer creation
- [ ] Manual release creation works via workflow_dispatch

---

Fixes #1

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commits.messageBody:
Adding CLAUDE.md with task information for AI processing.
This file will be removed when the task is complete.

Issue: https://github.com/VisageDvachevsky/veil-windows-client/issues/1

- Add file path fields for client.key and obfuscation.seed files
- Add browse buttons for file selection with file dialogs
- Implement file validation to check if files exist and are readable
- Add persistence using QSettings for all configuration options
- Add getter methods for key file and obfuscation seed paths

This matches the CLI client functionality which uses these files for:
- client.key: Pre-shared key for secure handshake authentication
- obfuscation.seed: Traffic morphing seed for DPI evasion

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.messageHeadline: Add cryptographic settings section to GUI settings widget

commits.messageBody:
- Create SVG icons for different connection states:
  - icon_disconnected.svg: Gray shield outline for disconnected state
  - icon_connecting.svg: Blue/cyan shield for connecting state
  - icon_connected.svg: Green filled shield with checkmark for connected state
  - icon_error.svg: Red shield with X for error state
- Add Qt resource file (resources.qrc) to embed icons
- Update CMakeLists.txt to include resources and optional Qt6::Svg
- Set window icon in MainWindow

The icons follow the VEIL design language with a shield motif
representing security, and the "V" letter inside representing VEIL.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.messageHeadline: Add SVG icons for VEIL VPN application

commits.messageBody:
- Add support for version tags (v*) to trigger releases
- Add workflow_dispatch for manual release creation
- Generate proper version strings from tags or timestamps
- Create zip archives of Windows builds for portable distribution
- Add detailed release body with installation instructions
- Support both tag-based releases and dev prereleases
- Mark manual/dev builds as prereleases

This enables:
1. Automatic releases when pushing tags like v1.0.0
2. Manual release creation via GitHub Actions UI
3. Proper versioning for all release artifacts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.messageHeadline: Improve CI release workflow for GitHub releases
Remove CLAUDE.md (not part of the solution)

commits.messageBody:
This commit adds several improvements to the VEIL VPN application:

Installer Improvements (NSIS):
- Detect existing installations and offer upgrade vs fresh install
- Version comparison to prevent downgrades without warning
- Backup settings before upgrade option
- Preserve QSettings during upgrades
- Stop running instances before upgrade
- Add version info to executable metadata
- Add Russian language support
- Create version.txt in install directory

Auto-Update Checker (GUI):
- New UpdateChecker class that uses GitHub Releases API
- Checks for updates on startup (3 second delay)
- Manual check via Help menu "Check for Updates"
- Shows dialog when update is available with download link
- Version comparison supporting semver with prerelease suffixes

Version Management:
- New src/common/version.h with compile-time version constants
- Central location for version numbers
- GitHub repository URLs for update checks

CI/CD Release Workflow:
- New comprehensive release.yml workflow
- Builds Windows and Linux artifacts
- Creates Windows installer with NSIS
- Creates Linux DEB packages and tarballs
- Generates changelog from git history
- Creates draft GitHub releases with proper assets

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.messageHeadline: Add installer upgrade support, auto-update checker, and release workflow

commits.messageBody:
- Remove qt6-networkauth-dev from linux-build.yml and release.yml
  (package not available on Ubuntu 22.04 and not used in codebase)
- Fix VEIL_THREAD_CHECKER macro to expand to static_assert in release
  builds, avoiding unused private field warnings with -Werror

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.messageHeadline: Fix Linux build failures on Ubuntu 22.04 and 24.04

title: Fix GUI clients: Add crypto settings, icons, and CI release workflow
